data.title: Hexagon reports 4% organic revenue growth in Q3 2025, with net sales of 1,303.8 MEUR.
data.description: In the third quarter of 2025, a 4% organic revenue growth was reported when using fixed exchange rates and a comparable group structure. Net sales saw an increase of 0% to reach 1,303.8 million euros (compared to 1,299.8 million euros). When factoring in the reduction of acquired deferred revenue, net sales totaled 1,303.1 million euros.
Bekaert provides update on share buyback program and liquidity agreement.

data.title: WLFI token of World Liberty Financial sees 30% drop in 2 weeks, might dip below $0.1. Investors worry about project's future.
data.description: The cryptocurrency token WLFI issued by World Liberty Financial has seen a significant 30% decrease in value over a span of two weeks. This decline has raised worries among investors about the possibility of the token falling below the $0.1 mark. Many investors have voiced their disappointment with the slow advancement of the project.
Bank of Åland Plc announces changes in Group Executive Team. Key numbers undisclosed.

data.title: Debate escalates over tokenized gold's blockchain incorporation with Binance's CZ questioning its legitimacy, sparking discussion on third-party custody.
data.description: CZ, the CEO of Binance, has questioned the validity of tokenized gold, sparking a discussion on its blockchain authentication and dependency on third-party custody. This has raised concerns in the cryptocurrency community regarding the true nature of gold-backed tokens, with many emphasizing the importance of transparency and security in such assets. The debate has garnered attention due to the increasing popularity of tokenized assets, with the issue highlighting the need for stringent verification processes and regulations to safeguard investors. The discussion has also underlined the significance of reliable custody solutions and blockchain infrastructure in ensuring the credibility and integrity of tokenized gold.

data.title: Ex-World Bank President urges U.S. to take lead in stablecoin innovation against global rivals; mentions Europe and China.
data.description: Former World Bank president David Malpass highlights the importance of the United States taking the lead in stablecoin innovation in the face of competition from Europe and China on a global scale.

data.title: Circus SE secures 6 new defense patents for autonomous AI robotics system CA-M in security and military ops.
data.description: Circus SE, a company listed on XETRA as CA1 and WKN A2YN35, recently disclosed that they have filed six innovative patents related to defense and autonomous AI robotics. These patents focus on the fundamental technologies underlying their autonomous AI robot system CA-M, which is designed for use in security-sensitive and military settings. The patents notably cover various aspects of autonomous food preparation by the AI robot CA-M. This development marks a significant stride in Circus SE's efforts to advance their technologies in these critical sectors.

data.title: Thales named Frost & Sullivan’s 2025 Company of the Year in Automated Border Control for expertise, customer focus, and modular systems.
data.description: In MEUDON, France, Thales is offering solutions to assist governments and airports in managing increasing passenger numbers, complying with regulations like the European Entry Exit System (EES), and addressing security risks. Frost & Sullivan recognized Thales for its comprehensive capabilities in creating systems that prioritize robust security measures while also enhancing passenger convenience. They particularly highlighted Thales's proficiency in developing modular, environmentally friendly systems incorporating advanced biometrics and artificial intelligence.

data.title: Rio Tinto announces Board changes, 2 members step down post 2025 AGMs, transitioning to smaller Board size.
data.description: In London, Rio Tinto has disclosed various changes to its Board. The Board, which had reached a peak size of 14 Directors for a transitional phase to blend the experience of longer-serving members with newer ones, has now undergone modifications. Following the departure of Sam Laidlaw and Kaisa Hietala after the 2025 AGMs, the Board has officially finalized its transitional phase. The number of Directors currently stands at [number], marking a reduction from its previous size. This development highlights the company's strategic shift towards a leaner and more focused leadership structure.
